content.problemStatement:
After six days, professor GukiZ decided to give more candies to his students. Like last time, he has NNN students, numbered 111 through NNN. Let's denote the number of candies GukiZ gave to the iii-th student by pip_ipi‚Äč. As GukiZ has a lot of students, he does not remember all the exact numbers of candies he gave to the students. He only remembers the following properties of the sequence ppp:


The numbers of candies given to each of the first KKK students (p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pKp_1, p_2, \dots, p_Kp1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pK‚Äč) are known exactly.
All elements of the sequence ppp are distinct and positive.
GukiZ didn't give more than xxx candies to any student (the maximum value in the sequence ppp is not greater than xxx).
For each student iii, there is at least one other student jjj such that ‚ą£pi‚ąípj‚ą£‚Č§D|p_i - p_j| \le D‚ą£pi‚Äč‚ąípj‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§D.
The professor gave out the biggest possible total number of candies, i.e. S=p1+p2+p3+‚Ä¶+pNS = p_1 + p_2 + p_3 + \ldots + p_NS=p1‚Äč+p2‚Äč+p3‚Äč+‚Ä¶+pN‚Äč is maximum possible.
GukiZ would like to know the total number of candies SSS he had at the beginning. However, times change and after six days, the professor is really tired, so it is possible that there is no sequence ppp which satisfies the constraints. Can you help GukiZ find the number of candies he gave out, or tell him that he must have made a mistake?
constraints: 1‚Č§pi‚Č§x1 \le p_i \le x1‚Č§pi‚Äč‚Č§x for each valid iii
All values pip_ipi‚Äč from input are distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers NNN, KKK, xxx, DDD.
The second line contains KKK distinct space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pKp_1, p_2, \dots, p_Kp1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pK‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of candies GukiZ had, or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if there is no valid sequence ppp.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are four students. We know that the first student got p1=2p_1 = 2p1‚Äč=2 candies, the second student got p2=1p_2 = 1p2‚Äč=1 and the third got p3=5p_3 = 5p3‚Äč=5 candies; we don't know the number of candies given to the last student. The maximum possible amount of candies given to some student is x=5x=5x=5. The best possible option is giving p4=4p_4=4p4‚Äč=4 candies to the last student. Then, the fourth constraint (with D=3D=3D=3) is satisfied for all students. Only the pair of students (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) have numbers of candies that differ by more than 333, but still, for each student, there are at least two other students with close enough numbers of candies.
Example case 2: GukiZ made some mistake in distribution and there is no valid sequence ppp. The answer is ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
